access.anon: Can query the public academic catalog and bus timetable through /api/graphql.
access.user: Can use personal mutations through a trusted same-origin session in addition to the public query surface.
access.admin: Uses the same ordinary personal mutation surface; GraphQL does not expose admin-only mutations.
access.agent: Can call personal mutations with a bearer token issued for the GraphQL resource and the mutation's feature:write scope. Authenticated MCP resources expose the SDL and persisted-operation manifest, but arbitrary GraphQL execution is not exposed as an MCP tool.

rules.scoped-mutations: Every personal mutation requires authentication, the matching feature:write OAuth scope for bearer callers, and the shared per-user mutation rate-limit action key used by REST and MCP. Trusted session callers receive the equivalent first-party access.
rules.single-endpoint: GraphQL HTTP requests use /api/graphql with GET, POST, and OPTIONS handlers.
rules.production-discovery: GraphiQL and schema introspection are disabled in production.
rules.request-budgets: Requests are limited to 64 KiB, one operation per HTTP request, five seconds, depth 8, 10 top-level fields, 15 aliases, 10 directives, 1000 tokens, and cost 5000; operation cost weights every paginated selection by its effective pageSize, including values supplied through variables.
rules.pagination: Page inputs default to page 1 and pageSize 20; pageSize must be between 1 and 100.
rules.resolver-boundary: Resolvers call feature-owned services only; they do not issue internal HTTP requests or access Prisma directly.
rules.request-cache: Entity DataLoaders are request-scoped and responses are marked no-store.
rules.safe-errors: Expected authentication, authorization, validation, not-found, rate-limit, and availability errors retain stable codes; unexpected resolver failures use Yoga error masking and must not expose internal exception details.
rules.zoned-datetime: DateTime inputs accept only valid ISO 8601 datetimes with an explicit Z or numeric timezone offset, with identical coercion for variables and inline literals.
rules.schema-evolution: The canonical SDL snapshot is checked for drift and breaking changes in CI.

public-query-endpoint.title: Public Queries and Scoped Personal Mutations

fields: Public semester, course, section, teacher, and bus queries
Authenticated todo, homework completion, section subscription, dashboard pin, bus preference, and comment mutations
GraphQL-audience bearer tokens require the matching feature:write scope
